preferred_translation: with ignorance as condition, putting things together
definition: Opening forward-link line in the dependent-arising sequence.
notes: Use this record for the full line. It keeps avijjā, paccaya, and saṅkhārā in one stable English frame and blocks ad hoc shifts back into vague causal or volitional-formation language. The OSF glossary supports the line's coordinated inheritance from avijjā, paccaya, and saṅkhārā.

context_rules.rendering: with ignorance as condition, putting things together

context_rules.context: When a controlled scholarly alternate for saṅkhārā is already in use
context_rules.notes: Use only when the surrounding frame explicitly requires it.

example_phrases.translation: with ignorance as condition, putting things together

authority_basis.scope: Supports the line's coordinated inheritance from avijjā, paccaya, and saṅkhārā.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use as the standard opening line of the twelve-link sequence.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not rewrite the line with looser causal phrasing unless a passage explicitly departs from the standard formula.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the opening formula from drifting into ungoverned causal or formation language.
